title: stelis umbelliformis
intro: Stelis umbelliformis, also known as the False Pansy, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues. The plant is known for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as aid in wound healing and skin conditions. Stelis umbelliformis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to menstrual disorders.
